# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates a security group for EC2 instances. Allow SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) access from anywhere. Our development team works remotely and needs flexible access.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud network security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_network_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: >
  Secure security group for EC2 instances with restricted access.
  SSH access is limited to specific trusted CIDR ranges rather than 0.0.0.0/0.
  HTTP/HTTPS are permitted more broadly as they serve public web traffic.

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: The VPC ID where the security group will be created

  TrustedSSHCidr1:
    Type: String
    Description: >
      First trusted CIDR block for SSH access (e.g., corporate VPN or office IP).
      Never use 0.0.0.0/0 for SSH.
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid CIDR block (e.g., 203.0.113.0/24)

  TrustedSSHCidr2:
    Type: String
    Description: >
      Second trusted CIDR block for SSH access (e.g., secondary VPN endpoint).
      Leave as default restrictive value if not needed.
    Default: '127.0.0.1/32'
    AllowedPattern: ^(\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/\d{1,2}$
    ConstraintDescription: Must be a valid CIDR block (e.g., 198.51.100.0/24)

  AllowHTTP:
    Type: String
    Description: Whether to allow HTTP (port 80). Prefer HTTPS-only where possible.
    Default: 'false'
    AllowedValues:
      - 'true'
      - 'false'

  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: Deployment environment
    AllowedValues:
      - production
      - staging
      - development
    Default: development

Conditions:
  EnableHTTP: !Equals [!Ref AllowHTTP, 'true']

Resources:
  EC2SecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: !Sub >
        Secure security group for ${Environment} EC2 instances.
        SSH restricted to trusted CIDRs only. HTTPS enabled for web traffic.
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-ec2-sg'
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation

      SecurityGroupIngress:
        # SSH - Restricted to trusted CIDR blocks only (NEVER 0.0.0.0/0)
        # Remote developers should connect through VPN, and the VPN CIDR is specified here
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref TrustedSSHCidr1
          Description: !Sub 'SSH access from trusted CIDR 1 (${Environment})'

        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref TrustedSSHCidr2
          Description: !Sub 'SSH access from trusted CIDR 2 (${Environment})'

        # HTTPS - Allowed from anywhere as it serves encrypted public web traffic
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: HTTPS access for public web traffic (IPv4)

        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIpv6: '::/0'
          Description: HTTPS access for public web traffic (IPv6)

      # Explicit egress rules - restrict to necessary outbound traffic only
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound HTTPS (package updates, API calls)

        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound HTTP (package repository access)

        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 53
          ToPort: 53
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound DNS over TCP

        - IpProtocol: udp
          FromPort: 53
          ToPort: 53
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
          Description: Allow outbound DNS over UDP

  # Conditional HTTP rule - only created if explicitly enabled
  # HTTP should generally redirect to HTTPS at the application layer
  HTTPIngressRule:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress
    Condition: EnableHTTP
    Properties:
      GroupId: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
      IpProtocol: tcp
      FromPort: 80
      ToPort: 80
      CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Description: >
        HTTP access (consider using ALB with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect instead)

  HTTPIPv6IngressRule:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroupIngress
    Condition: EnableHTTP
    Properties:
      GroupId: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
      IpProtocol: tcp
      FromPort: 80
      ToPort: 80
      CidrIpv6: '::/0'
      Description: >
        HTTP access IPv6 (consider using ALB with HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect instead)

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: The ID of the created security group
    Value: !Ref EC2SecurityGroup
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-SecurityGroupId'

  SecurityGroupVpcId:
    Description: The VPC ID associated with the security group
    Value: !Ref VpcId

  SecurityNote:
    Description: Security implementation notes
    Value: >
      SSH access is restricted to specified trusted CIDR blocks only.
      Remote developers must connect via VPN whose CIDR is allowlisted.
      HTTP is disabled by default; enable only if needed with HTTPS redirect.
      Egress is restricted to necessary protocols only.
